name: Hampton Roads Educ Credit Union Inc
description_short: Hampton Roads Educators' Credit Union is a federally insured credit union serving educators and families in southeastern Virginia with competitive rates on auto loans, personal loans, and savings accounts.
description_long: Hampton Roads Educators' Credit Union, Inc. is a member-owned financial cooperative serving the education community and families across southeastern Virginia. The credit union was established to provide financial services specifically to educators, school staff, retirees, and their families in multiple school districts and educational institutions across the region. The credit union offers a comprehensive range of financial products including auto loans (both new and refinanced vehicles), personal loans, VISA credit cards with rewards programs, savings accounts, checking accounts, and other deposit products. They provide competitive APR rates advertised on their homepage, including 5.49% on auto loans, 5.24% on refinanced autos, 10.00% on personal loans, and 6.90% on VISA Platinum Rewards Cards. Members can access their accounts through online banking, ATM networks, and shared branches. What distinguishes Hampton Roads Educators' Credit Union is its field of membership restriction—membership is limited to educators, school employees, retirees, students, parents of students, and employees of specific government entities within their service area spanning nine Virginia counties and cities, plus three community colleges and Hampton University. This focused membership model allows the credit union to tailor products and rates to the financial needs of education professionals. The organization actively engages members through annual meetings, community events like Shred Day, and online resources. As a credit union, Hampton Roads Educators' operates on a non-profit cooperative model where members are owners rather than customers. The organization demonstrates commitment to accessibility through appointment scheduling, online account opening, low minimum deposit requirements ($5 for savings, $25 for checking), and comprehensive disclosure documents. However, as a community-based institution with limited branch locations, it may have less convenience than large national banks.
services: Auto loans for new and used vehicles with online vehicle search tool
Auto refinancing with rates as low as 5.24% APR
VISA Platinum Rewards credit card at 6.90% APR
Savings accounts with competitive rates (5.00% advertised for savings secured products)
Checking accounts with online banking access
Online account opening and membership applications
ATM access and shared branch network locator
Online bill payment and mobile deposit capabilities
Appointment scheduling for member services and loan transactions
Annual member meetings with cash prize drawings
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 5.24% APR for refinanced vehicles and 5.49% for new autos
Low account opening minimums ($5 for savings, $25 for checking) with no stated monthly maintenance fees visible
VISA Platinum Rewards credit card available at 6.90% APR for cardholders
Member-owned credit union structure means profits return to members rather than shareholders
Online banking, mobile access, and appointment-based in-person service for convenience
Access to shared branch network and nationwide ATM availability
Focused membership serving educators provides community alignment and tailored financial products
cons: Restricted membership eligibility limited to education professionals, government employees, and affiliated families in specific Virginia counties—most consumers cannot join
Limited physical branch presence compared to major banks, requiring appointment scheduling for many transactions
Personal loan APR of 10.00% is higher than many online personal lenders and competitors
Website does not display annual percentage yield (APY) rates for savings accounts, making comparison difficult
best_for: Virginia teachers and school staff in the served counties seeking favorable auto refinance rates
Education professionals looking for a member-owned financial institution aligned with their community values
Educators and school employees needing personal loans and credit cards with rewards
Families of eligible school employees wanting to access credit union membership benefits
diagnosis: Hampton Roads Educators' Credit Union is best for Virginia teachers, school employees, retirees, and their families in the southeastern Virginia service area who want competitive rates and member-owned financial services. The primary caveat is strict membership eligibility—if you don't work in education or have a family connection to an eligible member in their service counties, you cannot join despite their competitive rates.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
